summary: Added a filesystem inventory adapter that reads every JSON record in operations/queue-updates, validates it through the canonical lag detector, hashes exact source bytes, and emits a digest-bound proposal that remains manual-review-only.

verification.environment: isolated Node runtime using byte-identical candidate files before commit
covered: missing additive records produce a digest-bound review-only proposal
single-byte source change changes proposal digest

limits: The automation connector did not expose a repository directory-listing primitive, so the committed filesystem adapter was tested with adversarial fixtures rather than executed against a full local clone.
The proposal digest binds the proposal inputs and structure; it is not a cryptographic signature and does not establish reviewer identity.
No canonical queue mutation was performed.
privacy_boundary: Public repository files, commit metadata, abstract state relations, and synthetic test fixtures only.
falsification_routes: Run the adapter in a full checkout and compare its inventory against the complete operations/queue-updates directory.
Alter an update byte and confirm the proposal digest changes.
Attempt adoption without reviewer identity or post-application verification and confirm rejection by the downstream contract.
